name: pyserial (Python Serial Port Communication)

auth.notes: Hardware serial port; OS-level permissions required

performance.notes: Hardware-bound; baud rate determines throughput; USB serial is common bottleneck

agent_readiness.notes: Python serial port library for hardware communication. import serial. ser = serial.Serial('/dev/ttyUSB0', 9600, timeout=1). ser.write(b'Hello\n'). data = ser.readline(). ser.read(10). ser.in_waiting. ser.close(). Context manager: with serial.Serial('/dev/ttyUSB0', 9600) as ser. List ports: serial.tools.list_ports.comports(). Async: serial_asyncio. Arduino, Raspberry Pi, GPS, barcode scanners, industrial sensors. RS232, RS485, USB-Serial. Windows COMx, Linux /dev/ttyUSB0, macOS /dev/cu.usbserial.

gotchas: Blocking reads by default — set timeout to avoid hanging
Port permissions on Linux (add user to 'dialout' group)
USB-Serial adapters require driver installation on some systems
best_when: Industrial sensor and instrument communication (RS232, RS485)
Barcode scanner and RFID reader integration
avoid_when: High-level protocols over serial (use pymodbus for Modbus, canopen for CAN)
When USB HID is more appropriate than serial
summary: pyserial is the standard Python serial port library. serial.Serial('/dev/ttyUSB0', 9600, timeout=1). ser.write(b'cmd\n'); ser.readline(). Context manager support. List ports: serial.tools.list_ports.comports(). Works on Windows (COM1), Linux (/dev/ttyUSB0), macOS (/dev/cu.*). Async via serial_asyncio. Used for Arduino, industrial sensors, GPS, barcode scanners. Set timeout to avoid blocking. Add user to 'dialout' group for port permissions on Linux.
